name: Wisconsin FoodShare (SNAP)
description: Wisconsin's SNAP program, called FoodShare, provides monthly food benefits on a QUEST EBT card. Benefits help low-income individuals and families purchase groceries at authorized retailers and farmers markets.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% FPL for most households

name: Wisconsin BadgerCare Plus (Medicaid)
description: Wisconsin's Medicaid program providing health coverage to eligible low-income residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, mental health, dental, vision, and long-term care services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant individuals, and caretaker relatives
incomeLimitNotes: Adults without children up to 100% FPL; children up to 300% FPL; pregnant individuals up to 300% FPL

name: Wisconsin Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Benefits are calculated based on previous wages and can last up to 26 weeks.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own with sufficient earnings history
incomeLimitNotes: Weekly benefit based on previous wages; maximum weekly benefit applies

name: Wisconsin Housing and Economic Development Authority (WHEDA)
description: Provides affordable housing programs including homebuyer assistance, rental assistance, and affordable housing development financing. Helps first-time homebuyers with down payment and closing costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income homebuyers and renters in Wisconsin
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program and county; generally targets households below 80% of area median income

name: Legal Action of Wisconsin
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents across Wisconsin. Handles cases involving housing, family law, public benefits, consumer protection, and employment.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Wisconsin residents with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Wisconsin Job Centers
description: Statewide network of workforce development centers providing free employment services including job search, career counseling, resume writing, skills assessments, and training opportunities.
eligibilitySummary: All Wisconsin job seekers and employers

name: Wisconsin Home Energy Assistance Program (WHEAP)
description: Provides financial assistance to low-income households to help pay heating and electric bills. Includes regular energy assistance, crisis assistance, and furnace repair/replacement.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 60% of state median income
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 60% of state median income

name: Wisconsin 211
description: Free, confidential service connecting Wisconsin residents to local resources for food, housing, health care, utilities, and other essential services. Available 24/7 by phone and online.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Wisconsin seeking information about community resources
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, Hmong, and interpreter services available

name: Feeding America Eastern Wisconsin
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving 36 counties in eastern Wisconsin. Distributes food through a network of over 500 partner agencies including food pantries, meal programs, and shelters.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in eastern Wisconsin

name: Second Harvest Foodbank of Southern Wisconsin
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving 16 counties in southern Wisconsin. Distributes food through a network of partner agencies to individuals and families facing food insecurity.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in southern Wisconsin

name: Feed My People Food Bank
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving 14 counties in western Wisconsin. Distributes food through partner agencies and mobile food pantry programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in western Wisconsin

name: Wisconsin Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Provides services to Wisconsin veterans including benefits claims assistance, home loans, education grants, employment support, and access to state veterans homes and cemeteries.
eligibilitySummary: Wisconsin veterans and their dependents

name: Wisconsin Crisis Line
description: Provides 24/7 crisis support for anyone in Wisconsin experiencing mental health crisis, suicidal thoughts, or emotional distress. Connects callers with local crisis intervention services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Wisconsin experiencing a mental health or substance use crisis
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Wisconsin Shares Child Care Subsidy
description: Provides child care subsidies to eligible families to help pay for child care while parents work or participate in Wisconsin Works (W-2) activities. Covers licensed and certified providers.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children under 13 who are working or in approved activities
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Wisconsin Works (W-2)
description: Wisconsin's TANF program providing employment assistance and cash benefits to low-income parents. Emphasizes work participation through subsidized employment, community service jobs, and transitional placements.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income parents with dependent children who need employment assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by W-2 tier; very low income for cash benefits

name: Wisconsin WIC Program
description: Provides nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level

name: Milwaukee County Transit System (MCTS)
description: Operates bus service in the Milwaukee metropolitan area. Offers reduced fare programs for seniors, people with disabilities, and Medicare card holders through GO Pass and other fare programs.
eligibilitySummary: Milwaukee area residents; reduced fares for seniors, disabled, and Medicare holders
hours: Service varies by route; customer service Mon-Fri 6:00 AM - 7:00 PM

name: Madison Metro Transit
description: Operates bus service in the Madison metropolitan area. Offers reduced fare programs for seniors, people with disabilities, and low-income riders.
eligibilitySummary: Madison area residents; reduced fares for seniors, disabled, and low-income riders
hours: Service varies by route; customer service Mon-Fri 7:15 AM - 5:30 PM

name: Community Advocates - Milwaukee
description: Provides comprehensive services in Milwaukee including energy assistance, rent assistance, legal aid, mental health services, and advocacy for low-income individuals and families.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Milwaukee area

name: Wisconsin Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes including insulation, air sealing, and heating system repairs to reduce energy bills and improve home comfort.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters at or below 60% of state median income
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 60% of state median income

name: Wisconsin Division of Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides services to help people with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment. Includes career counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Wisconsin Grant Program
description: State need-based financial aid for Wisconsin residents attending eligible in-state colleges and universities. Awards are based on financial need and do not require repayment.
eligibilitySummary: Wisconsin residents attending eligible in-state institutions with financial need
incomeLimitNotes: Must demonstrate financial need as determined by FAFSA

name: Wisconsin Adult Education
description: Provides free classes for adults including GED/HSED preparation, English language learning, basic literacy, and workforce readiness at technical colleges across Wisconsin.
eligibilitySummary: Adults without a high school diploma or needing English language skills

name: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline - Wisconsin
description: Provides free, confidential support for anyone in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Call or text 988 to connect with a trained counselor 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, mental health crisis, or emotional distress
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: End Domestic Abuse Wisconsin Hotline
description: Provides crisis support, safety planning, and referrals for survivors of domestic violence. Connects callers with local shelters, legal advocacy, and support services across Wisconsin.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, dating violence, or sexual assault
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, Hmong, and interpreter services available

name: Milwaukee Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) to low-income families in Milwaukee. Manages public housing developments and rental assistance programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in Milwaukee
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 50% of area median income for Section 8

name: Wisconsin GI Bill
description: Provides education benefits for eligible Wisconsin veterans and their dependents including tuition remission at UW System schools and Wisconsin technical colleges.
eligibilitySummary: Wisconsin veterans with qualifying service and their dependents

name: Disability Rights Wisconsin
description: Provides free legal advocacy for people with disabilities in Wisconsin. Handles issues including abuse and neglect, discrimination, education, employment, housing, and access to services.
eligibilitySummary: People with disabilities in Wisconsin

name: FamilyWize Prescription Discount Card - Wisconsin
description: Free prescription discount card program available to all Wisconsin residents with no income requirements. Can save up to 80% on prescription medications at participating pharmacies.
eligibilitySummary: All Wisconsin residents; no income requirements or insurance restrictions

name: Dane County Human Services - Madison
description: Provides comprehensive human services to Dane County residents including behavioral health, economic assistance, juvenile justice, adult protective services, and aging programs.
eligibilitySummary: Dane County residents in need of social and health services
